_purpose: Mile 0.2 baseline freeze. This file is the FIXED LEFT SIDE of every H22 drop-week diff. Blueprint (docs/H22_AGENT_HARNESS.md) §9 runbook step 1 stops with status REFUSED if this file is absent or unparseable; step 3 (quarantine re-litigation) reads quarantine_snapshot.members from here and NEVER from memory; step 4 (sweep re-run) hash-checks the H22 connectivity catalog against connectivity_catalog.sha256 here before diffing. Every datum below was verified by the freezing agent's own read-only Read/Grep/Bash in the freeze dispatch — no value is copied from another document's assertion. Frozen values are re-derivable via the `evidence_command` beside each. Nothing here is re-run at drop time except the four probes named in `consumers`.

provenance.governing_gate: Two gates. (1) Freeze-time: human merge-to-main of this artifact (per blueprint §6 acceptance — MODE A workflows may write only docs/, harness/notes/, harness/state/leg0_baselines.json, README.md; promotion to master is the human). (2) Consume-time: drop-week runbook step 1 baseline check, executed by h22-gatewarden — emits `GATE VERDICT: ALLOW` only if BOTH harness/state/drop.json AND this file exist and parse; otherwise the runbook returns status REFUSED and nothing runs.
provenance.relay: Produced in Leg 0. Consumed in Leg 1+ by the h22-drop-week runbook (steps 1, 3, 4). Frozen against the pre-drop H21.0.671 runtime; the RIGHT SIDE of every drop diff is the live H22 runtime.
provenance.frozen_at_commit_subject: release: v5.22.0 — the honest envelope · evidence-based anchors · drop-day runbook
provenance.author: h22-scribe
provenance.evidence_discipline: Every field verified by the author's own Read/Grep/Bash(read-only) this dispatch. Bash used only for sha256sum, json parsing, and git log -1 — no mutating command.

connectivity_catalog._what: Item (1) — catalog hash. The connectivity sweep catalog that runbook step 4 re-runs and diffs against on H22. Its sha256 is the tamper/staleness anchor: step 4 hash-checks the live H22 sweep's baseline against this value before trusting any diff.

symbol_table._what: Item (4) — sha256 of the committed dir() symbol-table authority. This is the membership authority that decides whether any hou.* symbol is present or phantom (per CLAUDE.md §11 rule 15 and checks.py::check_phantom_clean at checks.py:425). The whole-file sha256 is the drop-diff anchor; blake2b + symbol_count + houdini_version are the table's own internal integrity/version fields (read from inside the file, used by scout's loader).
symbol_table.internal_fields_evidence_command: python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('python/synapse/cognitive/tools/data/h21_symbol_table.json')); print(d['schema'], d['houdini_version'], d['symbol_count'], d['blake2b'])"
symbol_table.note: This is a HEADLESS-hython dir() table. GUI-only submodules (hou.ui, hou.qt, hou.audio, ...) and lazily-loaded namespaces are legitimately absent from it and are NOT phantoms — check_phantom_clean unions _GUI_HOU_ABSENT_HEADLESS to compensate (checks.py:444). Regenerate this table per Houdini build via host/introspect_runtime.py.

quarantine_snapshot._what: Item (2) — the exact confirmed-ABSENT symbol list and where it came from. Runbook step 3 (P6: the ONE legitimate re-probe event) reads members[] from here verbatim, probes each ONCE against the live H22 runtime, and re-pins each with an H22 tag. Membership is DECIDED by absence from the symbol_table above; the source enumerations below are where the harness codifies the list in prose.

governing_source.context: Filter 3 — Phantom gate. Verbatim: 'No confirmed-absent API on the critical path (`hou.pdg.*`, `hou.secure`, `hou.lopNetworks()`, `hou.updateGraphTick()` — auto-quarantined, never re-litigated).'

cross_references.form: check_phantom_clean enforces this membership on every sprint's ADDED .py lines using the symbol_table as authority.

quarantine_snapshot.note_form_divergence: Sources vary the call-form of the fourth symbol: SYNAPSE_ODFORCE_HARNESS.md and SYNAPSE_HARNESS_LAUNCH.md write `hou.updateGraphTick()` (parens); forge_mile2.md writes `hou.updateGraphTick` (no parens). Both refer to the same probed attribute `hou.updateGraphTick`. Frozen below by dotted attribute, call-form is cosmetic.
quarantine_snapshot.membership_authority: Absence from python/synapse/cognitive/tools/data/h21_symbol_table.json (symbol_count 33255, houdini_version 21.0.671). Each member below was checked present:false by the author this dispatch.

members.table_evidence_command: python -c "import json; s=set(json.load(open('python/synapse/cognitive/tools/data/h21_symbol_table.json'))['symbols']); print(any(x=='hou.pdg' or x.startswith('hou.pdg.') for x in s))"
members.note: The live PDG API is the top-level `pdg` module, NOT `hou.pdg.*`. Any `hou.pdg.<x>` is phantom. Stays dead on every D/O critical path (spec-D-diagnostic-truth.md:14). Re-litigate: low expectation of reappearance.

Absence here is NOT conclusive: hou.secure is a lazily-loaded namespace and a documented dir() blind spot (spec-0.2-api-delta-probe.md:50 — 'touch the lazily-loaded hou.qt/hou.text/hou.secure namespaces first'). Runbook step 3 states 'hou.secure PASS is significant: the auth resolver auto-adopts it.' Probe by importing the namespace, not by plain dir().
Known-phantom on H21; the LopNetwork walk-from-root idiom replaced it (CLAUDE.md R10 / shared/evolution.py::_sync_solaris_viewport).

test_pass_floor._what: Item (3) — the full `pytest tests/` GREEN floor, COPIED (not re-run) from harness/verify/suite_baseline.json. This is the ratchet floor read by checks.py::check_suite_baseline at merge-base(master, HEAD); a sprint may ratchet failures DOWN and passes UP but may never lower its own bar. Frozen here as the drop-week left side so a post-drop suite can be diffed against the pre-drop count.
test_pass_floor.source_commit_field: 178798d + D-track graft + crucible remediation (gate-AND, hython hard-miss)

consumers: runbook step 1 (h22-gatewarden): gate ALLOW requires this file to exist + parse — else REFUSED.
runbook step 3 (assayer): reads quarantine_snapshot.members[] verbatim, probes each ONCE on live H22, re-pins with H22 tag. hou.secure PASS auto-adopts the auth resolver.
runbook step 4 (sweep re-run): hash-checks the H22 connectivity baseline against connectivity_catalog.sha256 before diffing against verified_connectivity_21.0.671.json.
drop-week suite compare: post-drop full pytest diffed against test_pass_floor (passed 4118 / failed 0 / skipped 87).
non_goals: This file does NOT re-run the pytest suite — the floor is copied from suite_baseline.json and cited (item 3 is a copy-not-run by spec).
This file does NOT adjudicate the quarantine — it freezes membership + source. Re-litigation is runbook step 3's job on H22 (P6: the one legitimate re-probe event). Do not probe at freeze time.
This file does NOT regenerate the symbol table or the connectivity catalog — it hashes the committed H21.0.671 artifacts as-is. Regeneration is a drop-time host-layer probe.
This file is not a spec: it names no new design. It is a frozen evidence anchor. The four Phase-0 specs are Mile 0.3, written separately.
